metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten the proving command so it matches only markdown link occurrences of each filename (e.g. anchor the grep to link syntax), not any incidental prose mention.
findings.finding: The in-session completeness proving command (`grep -q "$b" docs/README.md`) checks only whether the filename STRING APPEARS ANYWHERE in the file — it passes even if the filename is mentioned only in prose (e.g., 'do not move bash-migration.md') rather than in an actual hyperlink. The AC claims 'every docs/*.md is LINKED from the index', but the command proves only 'mentioned', creating a false-positive risk.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — last bullet ('In-session completeness proof (single command)')
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the grep with one anchored to markdown link syntax:
```
for f in docs/*.md; do
  b=$(basename "$f")
  grep -qE "\(([^)]*/)?(${b})\)" docs/README.md || echo "UNLINKED: $b"
done
```
This matches `(path/to/filename.md)` — the href portion of a markdown link — so prose mentions without a hyperlink do not satisfy the check.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen the AC5 completeness proof (or add a companion check) to also verify that `dco-rollout-evidence.md`, `88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md`, and `bash-migration.md` appear under the 'Historical evidence' section heading — e.g., by extracting the section with `awk` and grepping within it, or by stating AC2 is manually verified via review of the diff.
findings.finding: The in-session completeness proof (AC5 shell one-liner) verifies only that each `docs/*.md` filename appears *somewhere* in `docs/README.md`, but does NOT verify that the three historical docs (`dco-rollout-evidence.md`, `88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md`, `bash-migration.md`) appear specifically inside the required 'Historical evidence' section (AC2). An implementer could satisfy the shell proof while placing historical docs in the wrong section, leaving the structural taxonomy requirement unverifiable by the stated acceptance test.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 (in-session completeness proof) vs. AC2 (Historical evidence section requirement)
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a companion verification step to AC5, e.g.:
```sh
# Verify historical docs are under the correct section:
awk '/^## Historical evidence/,/^## /' docs/README.md | grep -E 'dco-rollout-evidence|88ab-feature-branch-evidence|bash-migration' | wc -l
# must output 3
```
Alternatively, explicitly state in AC2 that section placement is verified by human review of the diff (acceptable since the file is tiny), so reviewers know not to rely on AC5 alone for this property.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: AC4 ('the docs-index CI gate... passes once wired') is not evaluable in-session for this ticket because f088 — which provides the CI gate — is explicitly not required to exist when this ticket is completed. This makes AC4 a deferred, external-dependency-gated criterion rather than an in-session verifiable one.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4: 'The index is complete and correct such that the docs-index CI gate added by sibling f088... passes once wired'

findings.finding: The plan says 'update inbound links' for the git-mv'd files but never enumerates or audits which files currently link to dco-rollout-evidence.md, 88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md, and bash-migration.md. Without an explicit audit step, inbound references in other docs (e.g., CLAUDE.md, other .md files, CI configs) will silently break after the move, stranding stale references.
findings.location: Scope section — 'move those files under docs/archive/ (git-mv, update inbound links)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add: 'Before relocating, run `grep -rn "bash-migration\|dco-rollout-evidence\|88ab-feature-branch" docs/ $(git ls-files)` and list every file with an inbound link; update each as part of this ticket.' Add those files to the Files section.

findings.finding: The plan asserts 'update inbound links' after git-mv of bash-migration.md to docs/archive/, but the actual inbound link count is large and unaudited: at least 10 references exist in src/ production docstrings (src/rebar/_lib_writes.py, _engine_support/validate.py, _engine_support/next_batch.py, _store/lock.py, _commands/composer.py, _commands/leaf.py, _commands/__init__.py, _commands/unlink.py, _commands/_seam.py), the root README.md, and docs/oss-comparison-and-remediation.md. The plan's scope bullet does not enumerate these, making it unverifiable whether the implementer will find and fix them all.
findings.location: Scope bullet: 'Candidates: dco-rollout-evidence.md, 88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md, bash-migration.md' / Files section
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit audit step: run `grep -r 'bash-migration.md' .` before the git-mv and list all hits as an AC sub-bullet. After the move, either (a) update every reference to the new path `docs/archive/bash-migration.md`, or (b) leave the file in place and only add the 'Historical evidence' section label in the index (no git-mv), since the file is already referenced by section-number anchors in production code.

findings.finding: No proving command is specified for any acceptance criterion. The only mentioned verifier — the docs-index CI gate from sibling ticket f088 — is explicitly not available during this ticket's execution ('does NOT require f088 to exist first'). There is no fallback shell command to confirm completeness of the index or correct link structure.

The plan says 'update inbound links' for the git-mv'd files but never enumerates the consumers outside docs/ that will need updating. bash-migration.md alone is referenced in 8+ src/rebar/ source-file docstrings, 3+ test files, root README.md, and docs/oss-comparison-and-remediation.md. 88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md is referenced in infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh. None of these cross-directory callers are listed in the plan's Files section.
findings.location: Scope section / Files list: 'plus relocated/updated: docs/dco-rollout-evidence.md, docs/88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md, docs/bash-migration.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the Files section to list: src/rebar/_commands/composer.py, src/rebar/_commands/leaf.py, src/rebar/_commands/unlink.py, src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py, src/rebar/_engine_support/validate.py, src/rebar/_engine_support/next_batch.py, src/rebar/_store/lock.py, src/rebar/_lib_writes.py, tests/integration/test_store_concurrency.py, tests/interfaces/queries/test_next_batch_compute.py, tests/interfaces/queries/test_validate_compute.py, tests/interfaces/facades/test_leaf_writes_parity.py, README.md, docs/oss-comparison-and-remediation.md, infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh — all require updating if bash-migration.md or 88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md are relocated.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
